items.question: When output feels generic, what should you fix first?
options: Switch to a newer model.
Clarify role, constraints, and desired output format.
items.theme_note: AI mirrors your inputs—upgrade structure before you blame the model.

items.question: You need a tight marketing line. Which style treats AI like a system, not a chat?
options: Hi! Please help me write a short line about our AI course. Thanks!
Task: 1 line (max 12 words). Product: AI course for consultants. Tone: direct.
items.theme_note: Stop saying please to your AI

items.question: Which prompt is more likely to produce a controlled first draft?
options: Write something about productivity.
Role: coach. Task: 3 bullet tips for managers. Max 80 words.

items.question: You want a usable LinkedIn post. Which prompt is better?
options: Write about AI for professionals.
Format: short. Audience: consultants. Goal: clicks. Tone: direct.
items.theme_note: Define the output—format, audience, goal—not just the topic.

items.question: Which approach scales better across repeated tasks?
options: Rewrite the whole prompt from scratch every time.
Reuse one structured template; swap only the inputs.
items.theme_note: Random one-off prompts → random results. Templates stabilize quality.

items.question: Which input is more likely to give a repeatable, controlled result?
options: Give me some productivity tips.
Role + task + output shape + constraints (length and tone).
items.theme_note: Move from conversation mode to configuration: role, context, output, limits.

items.question: Where does the biggest quality jump usually come from?
options: Trying more tools and toggling models.
One structured prompt: role, context, output, and constraints.
items.theme_note: Same model, better instruction—structure beats model-hopping.

items.question: Same task: a LinkedIn post about AI productivity. Which input wins?
options: Write a LinkedIn post about AI productivity.
Audience: managers. Goal: save time. Format: scannable. One concrete example.

items.question: What best describes serious prompting for real work?
options: Typing whatever comes to mind in the chat box.
Designing instructions: audience, success criteria, structure.
items.theme_note: Prompting is design, not casual typing.

items.question: Which pattern treats AI as a system, not a casual chat?
options: Can you please help me improve this text? Thanks!
Task: rewrite. Role: editor. Goal: clarity. Constraints: shorter, no fluff.
items.theme_note: From toy to system: specify task, role, goal, and constraints.
